operating_state: Revenue increased 45% to $65.2 billion driven by cardiometabolic health products, with Mounjaro and Zepbound together accounting for 56% of total revenues. Oncology revenue grew modestly led by Verzenio performance, while immunology reached strong levels with Taltz strength. International markets showed broadly positive momentum with particular strength in Europe and Japan, though CVS Caremark's removal of Zepbound from preferred formularies negatively impacted patient access.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved to 83.0% from 81.3% driven by favorable product mix toward higher-margin incretin medicines and improved production costs, partially offset by lower realized prices. Research and development expenses increased 21% including acquired IPR&D charges. Marketing, selling, and administrative expenses rose 29% with advertising costs doubling supporting cardiometabolic product launches. Manufacturing costs increased due to significant capacity expansion investments and supply chain fortification efforts.
material_events: Announced preliminary voluntary agreements with U.S. government including Medicare obesity medicine access by July 2026 and participation in government direct-to-patient purchasing platform. HHS selected Trulicity and Verzenio for government-set pricing effective 2028, following earlier Jardiance selection for 2026. Completed acquisitions including Scorpion Therapeutics and SiteOne Therapeutics. Repurchased $4.1 billion of shares under the $15.0 billion program and increased quarterly dividend to $1.73 per share. Kisunla received FDA approval for early symptomatic Alzheimer's disease treatment.
macro_exposures: Inflation Reduction Act implementation accelerated with additional product selections for government price-setting creating revenue erosion risk ahead of patent expiries. Foreign currency translation generated $1.2 billion positive impact from euro and yen strengthening. Cash payments of income taxes increased driven primarily by higher Ireland payments from increased production activity. New U.S. tariffs on Chinese goods with retaliatory Chinese tariffs create supply chain disruption risks, while potential BIOSECURE Act legislation threatens Chinese supply chain reliance.
forward_outlook: Management expects additional products will be selected for government-set Medicare pricing in future years, accelerating revenue erosion for affected medicines. Orforglipron regulatory submissions progressing with pre-launch inventory capitalized. Capital expenditures increased reflecting continued investment in manufacturing capacity. Strategic focus on direct-to-patient channels through LillyDirect expansion and alternative access strategies to mitigate formulary restrictions.
uncertainty_profile: Negotiation of definitive government pricing agreements presents risks of delays and unfavorable terms that could inadequately capitalize on expanded obesity medicine access. Patent litigation risks intensified with generic manufacturers increasingly challenging intellectual property across multiple jurisdictions. Multiple ongoing legal proceedings include insulin pricing litigation, 340B program disputes, and incretin medicine product liability cases. Manufacturing expansion commitments could require substantial payments if specified purchase amounts not met.
